key_points: Robert Arthur presents an unconventional secret agent in Ausable
Fowler’s expectations highlight common stereotypes about spies
The story is set in a hotel room in Paris, creating isolation and suspense
Max represents brute force and overconfidence
Ausable uses calmness and intelligence to control the situation
The imaginary balcony becomes a psychological trap for Max
Dialogue is the main tool used to build tension
The story focuses on psychological conflict rather than physical action
examples: Example of psychological control: Ausable calmly explains the balcony
Example of suspense: Max holding Ausable at gunpoint
Example of irony: Max trusting a story that leads to his death
literary_elements: Theme → Intelligence triumphs over physical force
Irony → A non-existent balcony causes a real tragedy
Conflict → Mental conflict between Ausable and Max
Characterisation → Contrast between Ausable and Max
exam_tips: Explain how Ausable’s calm behaviour changes the situation
Link Fowler’s role to the theme of appearance versus reality
Use the balcony incident as proof of irony
Mention dialogue when asked about suspense
Avoid describing the story as action-based; stress psychological tension
